text:
<think>I should begin by defining the concept of opportunity cost in clear and simple terms.</think>
Opportunity cost refers to the value of the next best alternative that must be forgone when making a choice.
<think>I should now elaborate on why opportunity cost is important in decision-making.</think>
This concept is important because resources such as time, money, and effort are limited, so choosing one option means giving up others.
<think>I need to provide a straightforward real-world example to illustrate the idea.</think>
For example, if you spend $20 on a movie ticket, the opportunity cost might be a meal you could have enjoyed with that money instead.
<think>I should present another example that demonstrates opportunity cost in a different context, such as time management.</think>
Another example is choosing to study for an exam rather than going out with friends; the opportunity cost is the enjoyment and social interaction you miss.
<think>Finally, I should summarize why recognizing opportunity costs leads to better decision-making.</think>
Understanding opportunity cost helps individuals and businesses make more informed and rational choices by considering what they must give up with each decision.
